_meta.note: annotation_ids reference IDs in web/lib/reports/rbi_sibc.ts. Nodes and edges are the new structured layer; annotations remain the source of interpreted content.

nodes._comment: ── DRIVERS — macro forces explaining why credit moves ──────────────────
nodes.label: GST / UDYAM Formalisation
nodes.description: Post-2018 GST and UDYAM registrations created a large cohort of businesses now visible to formal credit for the first time.

nodes.label: RBI Unsecured Risk-Weight Increase
nodes.description: Nov 2023: RBI raised risk weights on unsecured consumer credit from 100% to 125%, raising its capital cost and slowing origination.

nodes.label: Gold Price Appreciation
nodes.description: Gold prices rose ~25–30% over 2024–25, inflating collateral values for gold-backed lending and working capital for gold-dependent industries.

nodes.label: PLI Scheme / Manufacturing Capex
nodes.description: India's Production Linked Incentive scheme and supply chain localisation from China are generating new manufacturing investment and bank credit demand.

nodes.label: Household Liquidity Stress
nodes.description: Post-COVID income disruption and elevated inflation 2022–24 increased household demand for short-tenor liquidity against existing gold assets.

nodes.label: NBFC Intermediation Layer
nodes.description: NBFCs funded by banks extend credit to segments banks cannot cost-effectively reach: microfinance, small informal businesses, rural borrowers.

nodes.label: PSL Compliance Pressure
nodes.description: Banks must direct 40% of net bank credit to priority sectors. As total credit grows at 14.6%, PSL targets grow in absolute terms — harder to meet organically.

nodes._comment: ── GAPS — data limitations and blind spots ──────────────────────────────
nodes.label: Shipping: Reclassification, Not Collapse
nodes.description: ₹7.3L Cr → ₹0.1L Cr is a reporting change, not a credit contraction. Series is unusable until restated.

nodes.label: MSME Growth: Formalisation vs Real Demand
nodes.description: Part of +31.2% reflects newly-visible UDYAM-registered businesses, not businesses that were previously growing that fast.

nodes.label: PSL Lines Overlap — Do Not Sum
nodes.description: PSL sub-categories are not separate buckets. The same loan can appear in multiple PSL lines. Adding them overstates the total.

nodes.label: Multiple Series Broke in 2025
nodes.description: Shipping (Jan 2026), Renewable Energy (Mar 2025), Social Infrastructure (Mar 2025), and Advances against shares (Mar 2025) all show discontinuities that make YoY comparisons meaningless.

nodes.label: NBFC Retail Lending Not Visible Here
nodes.description: The ₹19.05L Cr is bank credit TO NBFCs (wholesale). What NBFCs lend to end borrowers — microfinance, small business, gold — is not in this dataset.

nodes.label: Gold Loan Products
nodes.description: ₹4L Cr growing +128.8%. Digital valuation and doorstep processing are the cost advantages. MSME gold-backed products are the high-velocity entry.

nodes.label: Medium Enterprise Lending
nodes.description: ₹4.26L Cr growing +22.3%. Too large for microfinance, too small for large corporate credit teams. Cash-flow underwriting, ticket ₹50L–₹5Cr.

nodes.label: Engineering Supply Chain Credit
nodes.description: PLI-linked companies expanding capacity. Supply chains verifiable via GST and TReDS. Long tenors, tangible collateral.

nodes.label: PSL Affordable Housing — Tier 3/4
nodes.description: PMAY demand exists. Constraint is distribution, not demand. 50 semi-urban clusters via BCs or co-lending generates business AND PSLC inventory.

nodes.label: IT / Software Working Capital
nodes.description: India's IT sector earns ₹22L Cr+ annually but has only ₹0.41L Cr in bank credit. Underleveraged relative to cash flows.

nodes.label: PSLC Origination — Sell Certificates
nodes.description: Banks buying PSLCs to meet targets creates a market. Lenders who can genuinely originate in weaker sections / affordable housing generate sellable inventory.

nodes._comment: ── PRESSURE POINTS — latent risks and stress ────────────────────────────
nodes.label: Gold LTV Risk at Scale
nodes.description: Most gold loans were originated near all-time high gold prices. A 20–30% price fall would breach LTV limits across the portfolio simultaneously, forcing auctions at scale.

nodes.label: NBFC Concentration in Bank Funding
nodes.description: ₹19.05L Cr of bank exposure growing +17.8% annually. Top 20 NBFCs are likely 80%+ of this. A single large NBFC stress event triggers contagion across bank credit.

nodes.label: Fintech Unsecured Book Not Visible
nodes.description: RBI's risk-weight action targeted fintech-originated short-tenor unsecured loans. This book is not in the RBI SIBC dataset — deterioration there would trigger another regulatory tightening.

edges.label: UDYAM-registered businesses becoming first-time bank borrowers
Makes it impossible to separate new credit demand from newly-visible businesses
Higher capital cost slowed origination and limit expansion
Unsecured credit repriced → borrower demand migrated to secured gold loans
Tighter unsecured lending environment accelerated fintech displacement of bank EMI
Higher gold prices raise eligible loan amounts against existing gold holdings
Gems & Jewellery working capital rises with gold inventory value
Households pledging gold to meet liquidity needs — not investment behaviour
PLI commitments require capex — companies borrowing to build capacity
Banks funding NBFC growth as indirect route to segments they can't serve
PSL housing qualifies for target fulfillment — compliance pressure is driving allocation
Loans originated at peak gold prices — LTV breach risk if prices fall 20–30%
Growing 4.4x with no deceleration — large headroom vs vehicle loans
MSME acceleration shows demand exists — Medium is fastest-growing, smallest stock
44.9% growth in 2 years at only 10% of industry stock — underserved window open
PLI companies expanding capacity need supply chain working capital — GST/TReDS verifiable
Policy momentum + PMAY demand exists — constraint is Tier 3/4 distribution, not demand
Banks buying PSLCs to meet targets — genuine PSL originators can sell certificates
₹19L Cr growing 17.8% annually — single NBFC stress cascades into bank books
₹7.3L Cr → ₹0.1L Cr is a classification change, not a real credit collapse
Credit card stall is visible — but the fintech unsecured book that replaced it is not in this dataset

edges._comment: ── Missing opportunity / risk / gap edges — added after validation ──────────
edges.label: IT/software is <1% of services credit but growing +20.7% — underleveraged relative to sector cash flows
RBI tightening displaced unsecured origination to fintechs — that book is now off-balance-sheet and invisible to this dataset
PSL categories are not separate buckets — same loan counted across multiple PSL lines; totals cannot be summed
Shipping, Renewable Energy, Social Infrastructure all show Mar 2025 discontinuities — reclassifications, not real credit moves
₹19.05L Cr is bank credit TO NBFCs — what NBFCs lend to end borrowers (microfinance, small biz, gold) is not in this dataset
Large credit +5.5% vs Micro & Small +31.2% — structural shift confirmed over 2 years
Retail now exceeds industry by ₹23.3L Cr and the gap is widening
